complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down this refactoring task into discrete, testable subtasks: (1) Merge conflict resolution per file, (2) Stub route removal/implementation strategy, (3) Winston logger setup and integration, (4) Dependency audit and removal, (5) .gitignore updates, (6) AI config consolidation, (7) Admin hook extraction per panel, (8) Type generation script setup, (9) ESLint naming audit, (10) Build and test validation. For each, define success criteria and testing approach.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task involves multiple independent but interconnected refactoring activities across different systems (git, logging, dependencies, configuration, code organization). The complexity stems from: (1) Merge conflict resolution requiring manual intervention and domain knowledge, (2) Deciding whether to remove or implement 501 stub routes (high-impact decision), (3) Integrating a new logging library across the codebase, (4) Dependency management with potential ripple effects, (5) Multiple file system and configuration changes. The test strategy is comprehensive but requires validation across build, runtime, and browser environments. Cyclomatic complexity is moderate per subtask, but the coordination overhead and number of distinct concerns elevates overall complexity[1][2].

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Decompose documentation creation into focused subtasks: (1) docs/ folder structure setup, (2) CONTRIBUTING.md with Git workflow, (3) DEVELOPMENT.md with local setup, (4) ARCHITECTURE.md with Mermaid diagrams, (5) API.md route documentation, (6) DATABASE.md schema documentation, (7) Bug report template, (8) Feature request template, (9) PR template with checklist, (10) GitHub labels and milestone creation, (11) README.md updates with badges and links. complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Documentation tasks are primarily linear and low-technical-complexity, but involve high volume and attention to detail. Complexity factors: (1) Multiple document types with different structures and audiences, (2) Mermaid diagram creation requires architectural understanding, (3) GitHub template integration requires platform-specific knowledge, (4) Validation across multiple rendering contexts (GitHub, Markdown preview). However, each subtask is largely independent and can be parallelized. The dependency on Task 26 is soft (refactoring should be complete for accurate documentation, but documentation can begin in parallel)[2].

The task is moderately complex due to the blend of automation and human judgment[1][3].

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Design Tokens in CSS/Tailwind Config
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break token implementation into systematic subtasks: (1) CSS variable definition in globals.css (semantic tokens), (2) Tailwind config theme extension (typography scale), (3) Tailwind config spacing scale, (4) Font setup via next/font/google, (5) Hardcoded color replacement audit and refactoring, (6) Focus-visible and accessibility ring styles, (7) Token audit script creation and validation. Prioritize by page/component impact.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Token implementation requires careful coordination between CSS, Tailwind configuration, and component usage. Complexity factors: (1) CSS variable naming and organization must be consistent and maintainable, (2) Tailwind config extension requires understanding of Tailwind's theme structure, (3) Replacing hardcoded values across the codebase is error-prone and requires systematic auditing, (4) Accessibility requirements (focus-visible rings) add validation overhead, (5) Token audit script must be comprehensive to catch all violations. The task is moderately complex due to the need for systematic refactoring and validation[1][2].
